# Our Story

Iterable’s core purpose has always revolved around people—enriching the relationships between consumers and brands, empowering the marketers who create experiences, and uniting a team of people capable of bringing such a vision to life. These are the key moments that have defined Iterable and continue to guide us along our journey.

![](https://iterable.com/wp-content/uploads/2026/04/collage-about-us-2025-1024x546.webp)

## Purpose

We exist to make meaningful customer connections possible for every brand.

## Leadership Team

![](https://iterable.com/wp-content/uploads/2026/04/sam-allen-2026-1024x1024.jpeg)

### Sam Allen

Chief Executive Officer

Joined Iterable in 2025. Previously served as EVP & Global COO at Tableau and as COO & GM at Salesforce.org, where he helped scale multi-billion-dollar businesses and led global operations, GTM strategy, and M&A. Brings over 30 years of experience driving transformation and growth across enterprise software, with a track record of building high-performing teams and delivering results at scale. MBA from the Wharton School and BA from the University of Missouri.

![](https://iterable.com/wp-content/uploads/2026/04/Andrew_Boni-1-1024x1024.webp)

### Andrew Boni

Founder and Chief Scientist  

Co-founded Iterable in 2013, and has served as CEO between 2021 and 2025. Prior to Iterable, Andrew worked on Google’s Strategic Partnerships team. Graduated with a B.S. in Computer Science from Boston College.  

*   LinkedIn

Chief Marketing Officer

Joined Iterable in 2025. Previously, Head of Marketing at SurveyMonkey, leading global brand, performance, content, and go-to-market strategy. 15+ years as a marketing leader driving growth and product innovation for leading B2B SaaS companies, including Box. BS in Computer Engineering from UC Irvine; MBA from UCLA.

Chief Product Officer

Joined Iterable in 2025. Previously, CTO of Tableau at Salesforce, leading AI-native analytics strategy and execution. Earlier, SVP and GM of Tableau Next, driving product innovation and go-to-market. Co-founder and President of Narrative Science, acquired by Salesforce in 2021. BS in Business Finance from the University of Missouri–Columbia.

Chief People and Culture Officer

Chief Financial Officer

Joined Iterable in 2025. Brings over 25 years of global finance leadership across high-growth enterprise software, including senior roles at Celonis, Oracle, and HP. Most recently served as SVP of Finance at Celonis, where he led corporate FP&A and global finance strategy. MBA from UC Berkeley Haas and BA from UC Davis.

Chief Information Security Officer

Over 15 years of experience in security, risk management, and information technology. BS in Mathematics from San Jose State University.

## Our Core Values

### Run As One

We run as one by anchoring our work in shared company outcomes, as laid out in the V2MOMs, clearly defining the end state before executing, and committing to decisions once they are made. This allows us to move quickly, operate independently, and scale without friction.

*   Company > Team > Self
*   Clarify the End State
*   Trust the Call

### Be An Owner

We embrace accountability. We act like owners, not employees, because that is what we are. We take responsibility for the company’s success as a whole, not just our individual roles. We win and fail (and learn) together. We exercise judgment, caring deeply about outcomes and making decisions as if the business were our own.

*   Own The Outcome, Not Just The Task
*   See It, Fix It
*   Spend Like It’s Yours

### Transparency

We build unbreakable trust by choosing clear communication over comfortable silence. We share our work early, own our mistakes, and have the courage to challenge one another directly with empathy.

*   Share Early and Often
*   Integrity Over Image
*   Challenge Directly, Care Personally

### Growth Mindset

We push beyond comfort zones—stretching, iterating, and learning faster than the challenges we face. Complacency is never an option. We question the status quo, extract lessons from every outcome, and adapt with humility and purpose. We know that leadership means modeling growth, not just expecting it.

*   Challenge the Status Quo
*   Lead through Learning
*   Seek the Stretch

## Discover What Makes Iterable a Best Place to Work

We believe contributions aren’t bound by location. Employees from all over the world have helped foster an inclusive remote work culture. Together, we’ve been building the operations, processes, and supporting structures that ensure our remote teams are empowered to thrive in the same collaborative and visible team environments as our physical office locations. Read more from our remote employees in this blog post.
